objects.name: Analytic 1730
objects.description: The defender correlates anomalous application package replacement, update, or executable-content drift with subsequent execution under the trusted application's identity, especially when package metadata, signing lineage, install source, file integrity, or native/DEX component characteristics change without a corresponding trusted distribution path. The analytic prioritizes Android-observable control-plane effects: package install/update events, package hash or code-section drift, signer mismatch or lineage break, unexpected app process behavior after replacement, and optional near-term network or sensor activity inconsistent with the legitimate application's baseline.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Managed application package version, signer lineage, installer source, or app identity changes outside approved enterprise or store-mediated update workflow

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Existing application is replaced, updated, or reinstalled and the resulting package metadata, code sections, or executable-supporting artifacts diverge from known-good baseline during the persistence-establishment phase

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: APK, DEX, native library, or package-associated executable content is written, expanded, or swapped in app package paths, staging paths, or installer cache immediately before or during application replacement

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Modified or newly replaced application begins execution or persists while recent_user_interaction=false or device_locked=true or launch context is inconsistent with expected user-driven update flow

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between package replacement, code drift, first launch, and follow-on behavior
Applications legitimately expected to update frequently or use staged package delivery
Expected install or update sources such as managed store, Google Play, or enterprise MDM
Approved signing certificates, rotation chains, and version lineage for managed apps
Expected package cache, installer, and app storage locations involved in legitimate updates
Degree of executable-content or metadata change tolerated before alerting
Whether package replacement and first launch should occur only during active user-driven workflows
Minimum outbound volume after first execution of replaced app to treat post-compromise communication as meaningful
